Package org.eventb.internal.pp.core

Class ClauseDispatcher

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.ClauseDispatcher
public final class ClauseDispatcher extends Object
This class decides in which order prover modules are invoked and manages derived and subsumed clauses.

The clause dispatcher maintains two data structures, one containing the clauses that have already been handled and dispatched to the modules (alreadyDispatchedClauses), and one containing clauses that have been derived by a prover module but not yet dispatched to the other modules (nonDispatchedClauses).

The algorithm works step-by-step. At each step, the dispatcher does the following.

  • It dispatches all non dispatched clauses to the prover modules using IProverModule.addClauseAndDetectContradiction(Clause).
  • It gets the next clause from one of the prover module using IProverModule.next(boolean).
Between those two, it checks whether a contradiction has been derived and if it is the case, it first call IProverModule.contradiction(Level, Level, Set) on all prover modules and then it backtracks its data structures (the already dispatched clauses and the non-dispatched clauses). Doing so, it calls IProverModule.removeClause(Clause) for each backtracked clause on each prover module. Before each steps, it checks whether the maximal number of steps has been reached or whether the user has canceled the proof, in which case it stops.

The prover modules never have in their internal state clauses that are not in the alreadyDispatchedClauses list of the clause dispatcher.

The clause dispatcher never has two clauses that are the same but with different levels in its state. It always removes the clause with the lowest level without adding the new one. It is therefore the same for prover modules. If it already exists, a clause is never added without first having been removed. This means that when the ClauseDispatcher calls IProverModule.addClauseAndDetectContradiction(Clause), either the given clause is new (has never been generated before), or it has already been generated but first removed using IProverModule.removeClause(Clause).

    • ClauseDispatcher

      public ClauseDispatcher(CancellationChecker cancellation)

  • Method Details

    • debug

      public static void debug(String message)

    • addProverModule

      public void addProverModule(IProverModule prover)
      Adds a prover module to the clause dispatcher.

      Prover module are invoked one after the other in the order they were added to derive new clauses out of the dispatched clauses.

      Parameters:
      prover - the prover module to add

    • setClauses

      public void setClauses(Collection<Clause> clauses)
      Sets the original clauses for this proof
      Parameters:
      clauses - the original clauses

    • addSimplifier

      public void addSimplifier(ISimplifier simplifier)
      Adds a simplifier.

      After a clause is derived by a prover module and before it is added to the list of clauses, simplifiers added using this method are called on the clause. They are called in the order in which they were added.

      Parameters:
      simplifier - the simplifier to add

    • getTracer

      public ITracer getTracer()
      Returns the tracer of this proof.
      Returns:
      the tracer of this proof

    • getLevelController

      public ILevelController getLevelController()
      Returns the level controller.
      Returns:
      the level controller

    • getResult

      public PPResult getResult()
      Returns the result of this proof.
      Returns:
      the result of this proof

    • mainLoop

      public void mainLoop(long nofSteps)
      Starts the clause dispatcher on the specified clauses.

      If a result is not found after the specified number of steps, the clause dispatcher stops.

      Parameters:
      nofSteps - the number of steps after which the clause dispatcher stops
